Fit Score: High — Riverside clears all three scoring axes at the highest level. Sector fit is HIGH: specialty manufacturing and value-added distribution are core, intentional focus areas — the active portfolio includes Seatex (chemical manufacturing platform, Oct 2024), Key Polymer (specialty adhesives/sealants/coatings manufacturer, Jan 2026 add-on), siffron (merchandising display manufacturer with global supply chain), U.S. Cabinet Depot (global sourcing/distribution), and Dastex (cleanroom consumables distributor with four active add-ons). **High fit confirmed.** The score is clean across all three axes — sector, deal activity, and access. The Key Polymer add-on closing just weeks ago (January 14, 2026) is a live, ticking integration clock. Pradeep Saha's direct email is on his personal website. firm_profile.fund_size: ~$14.1B AUM (total firm); Riverside Micro-Cap Fund VI: $1.872B (March 2023); Riverside Value Fund II: $750M hard cap (July 2025); Riverside Value Fund I: $350M (June 2023)
firm_profile.fund_vintage: Riverside Micro-Cap Fund VI, 2023; Riverside Value Fund II, 2025; Riverside Europe Fund VII, 2023 (in market)
firm_profile.investment_thesis: Riverside focuses exclusively on the smaller end of the middle market, targeting companies with generally up to $10M EBITDA (Micro-Cap) and up to $400M enterprise value (Capital Appreciation/Value funds). The firm pursues a buy-and-build strategy — sourcing 'little leading' companies and scaling them through organic growth and add-on acquisitions. For Specialty Manufacturing & Distribution, Riverside targets market-leading companies with 15%+ EBITDA margins, strong customer value propositions, and recurring-demand products. The Riverside Value Fund specifically targets companies in transition or at an inflection point — including carve-outs, family-owned transitions, and operational turnarounds — and applies hands-on operational strategies to accelerate revenue and earnings growth. Riverside's operating toolkit explicitly includes LEAN manufacturing, supply chain optimization, pricing improvement, and sourcing/procurement as value-creation levers.

recent_portfolio.name: Seatex
recent_portfolio.notes: Leading provider of chemical manufacturing services including complex blending, reactions, formulation and supply chain management. Headquartered in Rosenberg, TX. Riverside Value fund. Operating Partner Pradeep Saha involved. Add-on Key Polymer acquired Jan 2026.

recent_portfolio.name: Key Polymer
recent_portfolio.notes: Add-on to Seatex platform. Specialty manufacturer of high-performance adhesives, sealants and coatings. Two ISO 9001:2015-certified facilities in Lawrence, MA and Greenville, SC. Markets: industrial, automotive, aerospace, electronics, transportation manufacturing.

recent_portfolio.name: U.S. Cabinet Depot (USCD)
recent_portfolio.sector: wholesale distribution of specialty cabinetry & building products
recent_portfolio.notes: Leading wholesale distributor of ready-to-assemble kitchen & bath cabinetry. Manages global supply chain with multiple US distribution facilities. Riverside Value fund. Operating Partner Pradeep Saha involved.

recent_portfolio.name: siffron
recent_portfolio.sector: merchandising display & retail loss prevention manufacturing
recent_portfolio.notes: Designer, manufacturer and supplier of merchandising display and loss prevention solutions. Domestic manufacturing in Ohio and Illinois; international ops in China, Western Europe, Mexico, India. Riverside Value fund. Co-invested with Blue Point Capital Partners. Operating Partner Marc Jourlait involved.

recent_portfolio.name: Dastex
recent_portfolio.notes: European distributor/supplier of disposable/reusable cleanroom consumables (clothing, gloves, wipes, disinfection). Targets pharma, semiconductors, microelectronics, aerospace. Riverside Europe fund. Multiple add-ons: Conformat (2025), Pure11 (2025), AET (Apr 2024), Vita Verita (Jun 2023).

recent_portfolio.name: LMG Holdings, Inc.
recent_portfolio.sector: specialty manufacturing & distribution — ignition interlock devices
recent_portfolio.notes: Provider of ignition interlock devices. Specialty Manufacturing & Distribution + Business Services. Riverside Value fund. North America.

recent_portfolio.name: PFB Corporation (exited)
recent_portfolio.notes: Vertically integrated manufacturer of Expanded Polystyrene (EPS) insulation products. 8 manufacturing facilities in Canada + 3 in Midwest US. Riverside invested in automation and manufacturing capacity. EBITDA more than doubled; enterprise value tripled. Sold to Carlisle Companies for ~$260M in Q4 2024.

recent_portfolio.name: OnScent
recent_portfolio.notes: Partners with product innovators to create custom fragrances and natural cosmetic solutions. Riverside Micro-Cap fund. North America.

recent_portfolio.name: Essential Compositions
recent_portfolio.notes: Spanish developer and manufacturer of fragrances. Riverside's 6th deal in European specialty ingredients space. Riverside Europe fund. Part of Riverside 'specialty ingredients super-thesis.'

recent_portfolio.name: Precision-Engineered Manufacturers Platform (unnamed)
recent_portfolio.notes: Listed in Riverside Capital Appreciation portfolio as 'holding company for precision-engineered manufacturers.' Active, North America. Exact company name not disclosed on website.

recent_portfolio.name: CIS Secure Computing
recent_portfolio.notes: Designer and manufacturer of secure computing and communication solutions for government and commercial customers. Riverside Strategic Capital (Flexible Capital strategy).

recent_portfolio.name: Impact XM
recent_portfolio.notes: Full-service event and experiential marketing agency including fabrication and logistics. Riverside Value fund. Operating Partner Pradeep Saha involved.

operating_partners.name: Pradeep Saha
operating_partners.title: Operating Partner
operating_partners.background_summary: 25+ years driving double-digit growth across industrials, technology, and energy sectors. Former CEO of Horsburgh & Scott (leading gear manufacturer for steel and mining). Former CEO of Signature Systems Group. Led global engineering and sales for electric motors at Ametek. Early career at GE in multiple P&L and engineering leadership roles (nuclear, homeland security, flat panel displays). Expertise: Six Sigma green belt, LEAN, supply chain optimization, sourcing, sales & marketing, new product development. BS Electrical Engineering; MBA University of Toledo; executive education at Northwestern Kellogg and GE Crotonville. Active on Seatex (chemical manufacturing), US Cabinet Depot (distribution/supply chain), and Impact XM deals for Riverside.

operating_partners.name: Tom Barrett
operating_partners.title: Operating Partner
operating_partners.background_summary: 25+ years leading high-growth and turnaround strategies from startups to Fortune 500. Former President & CEO of 7SIGNAL (enterprise Wi-Fi software). Former Portfolio CEO at Brennan Management Group (family office owning education businesses, $130M revenue, 2,000 employees — led turnaround and sale). Former GM/Executive Director of Learn.com (sold to Taleo/Oracle). Founded North American Sales organization for iPass (grew to $100M recurring revenue, 2003 IPO). Experience in 2 IPOs and 5 M&A transactions. Placed by FALCON executive search. Serves as Executive Chair of multiple Riverside portfolio companies. Green Belt Value Stream Mapping certification; FCPA certification.

operating_partners.name: Michael Eblin
operating_partners.title: Senior Operating Partner
operating_partners.background_summary: Senior Operating Partner focused on Riverside's Franchising Specialization. Active on EverSmith Brands add-on deals. Background in franchise operations and business scaling. Works alongside Managing Partner Loren Schlachet on franchisor platform investments.

operating_partners.name: Mark Eblin
operating_partners.title: Operating Partner
operating_partners.background_summary: Operating Partner focused on Riverside's senior care and franchising portfolio. Active on Best Life Brands (Blue Moon Estate Sales, Next Day Access add-ons). Strategic execution and business acceleration focus. Based in Columbus, Ohio metro area.

operating_partners.name: Marc Jourlait
operating_partners.title: Operating Partner
operating_partners.background_summary: Global CEO and private equity operating partner background. Extensive international operating experience including P&L leadership across Americas, Europe and Asia. Education: ESCP Europe. Active on siffron (specialty manufacturing/distribution) deal for Riverside Value fund.

contacts.name: Pradeep Saha
contacts.title: Operating Partner
contacts.background_summary: 25+ years driving operational transformation across industrials, technology, and energy sectors. Former CEO of Horsburgh & Scott (a leading gear manufacturer serving steel and mining industries). Former CEO of Signature Systems Group. Led global engineering and sales for electric motors at Ametek. Early career at GE in multiple P&L and engineering leadership roles across nuclear, homeland security, and flat panel display divisions. Explicit operational toolkit includes: Six Sigma green belt, LEAN manufacturing, supply chain optimization, and sourcing — exactly the levers procurement consultants support. Active Operating Partner on Riverside's most relevant manufacturing deals: Seatex (chemical manufacturing, Oct 2024 platform) and its add-on Key Polymer (specialty adhesives/coatings, Jan 2026), plus US Cabinet Depot (global supply chain distribution, Feb 2024) and Impact XM. Sits on all boards as Operating Partner and is quoted in Riverside press releases as leading value creation. Based in Los Angeles (Santa Monica office).
contacts.recent_public_statements: Quoted in the January 2026 Key Polymer press release: 'Key Polymer has been successful at growing its business in recent years and brings many strengths to the combined organization including exceptional technical capabilities and a strong market position in the growing CASE (coatings, adhesives, sealants, elastomers) segment.' Also posted on LinkedIn celebrating his board appointment at Seatex: 'Excited to join the Board of Seatex as The Riverside Company Operating Partner. Looking forward to working with the team to drive this next phase of value creation.' His personal consulting website (pradeepsaha.com) lists 'supply chain optimization' and 'sourcing' explicitly as core operational competencies.
contacts.shared_context: Pradeep Saha's own website lists 'supply chain optimization' and 'sourcing' as explicit value creation levers — he has already internalized the language of procurement transformation. He is currently active on two fresh manufacturing platforms (Seatex + Key Polymer add-on completed January 2026) where procurement due diligence is immediately actionable across multi-site chemical manufacturing operations. His GE and Ametek background means he has lived inside procurement-heavy industrial environments and will speak the language of strategic sourcing fluently. His personal email and phone are publicly available on his consulting website, making this a genuinely warm outreach opportunity. Referencing the Key Polymer integration and the complexity of consolidating procurement across chemical manufacturing platforms is a natural conversation starter.

contacts.name: Marc Jourlait
contacts.title: Operating Partner
contacts.background_summary: Global CEO and PE operating partner with 30+ years of experience across Apple, HP, Seagate, Bose (VP/GM Europe), Navico (Deputy CEO, Altor PE-backed), and Kodak Alaris (CEO, turned around a post-bankruptcy spinoff achieving 27 consecutive months of beating EBITDA targets). Joined Riverside in 2020 as Operating Partner for the Riverside Value Fund. Currently serving as Chairman of SCRAM Systems (merger of LMG Holdings/LifeSafer and Alcohol Monitoring Systems) and active on the siffron deal (merchandising display manufacturer with domestic operations in Ohio and Illinois and international ops in China, Mexico, India, Western Europe). Has also served as Operating Partner for Oaktree Capital and Cathay Capital portfolio companies. Has triple citizenship (France, USA, Canada); lives in Manhattan. YPO Manhattan and YPO Gold Paris Chapter member.
contacts.recent_public_statements: Spoke at the Operating Partners Human Capital Forum (March 25–26, NYC) on the Ownership Works Program — Riverside's initiative to create employee-owners at portfolio companies. Quoted by Private Equity International: discussed talent marketplace, leadership team assembly, and value creation. In the Acertitude 'Brilliant People Podcast' interview (2023): 'In private equity value investing, our mission is to acquire undervalued assets and transform them for the better... At the heart of this process is people. My first order of business is to assess and assemble a capable team.' His primary focus is leadership/talent and organizational transformation rather than procurement specifically.
professional_interests: leadership team assembly and CEO coaching
contacts.shared_context: Although Marc's primary focus is leadership and organizational transformation rather than procurement specifically, his active role on siffron — a manufacturer with complex global supply chains spanning Ohio, Illinois, China, Mexico, India, and Western Europe — creates a natural entry point. A conversation framed around procurement complexity in multi-geography manufacturing operations, particularly the sourcing consolidation challenge when integrating add-on acquisitions, would resonate with his integration experience. His Manhattan base and YPO membership suggest a well-networked professional.

contacts.name: Craig Kahler
contacts.title: Partner, Riverside Value Fund
contacts.background_summary: 20+ years of middle-market private equity investing experience. Joined Riverside in August 2024 as a Partner on the Riverside Value Fund (RVF) team, working closely with Managing Partner Sean Ozbolt. Works alongside Pradeep Saha on manufacturing/specialty chemical deals. Named deal lead on the Seatex platform acquisition (Oct 2024, chemical manufacturing) and the Key Polymer add-on (Jan 2026, specialty adhesives/sealants/coatings). Quoted directly in the Key Polymer press release: 'This represents our first follow-on acquisition since we acquired Seatex in October 2024 and materially advances our plans to expand the end market and geographic reach of the platform.' Also named as deal lead on RVF deals such as US Cabinet Depot. Based in Los Angeles (Santa Monica office). Prior experience at Aurora Resurgence (PE, special situations).
contacts.recent_public_statements: Quoted in Key Polymer press release (January 14, 2026): 'Key Polymer is an excellent complement to the Seatex platform. This represents our first follow-on acquisition since we acquired Seatex in October 2024 and materially advances our plans to expand the end market and geographic reach of the platform.' Quoted in Craig Kahler hiring announcement (August 2024): 'I'm excited to join such a talented and focused team. I look forward to being a part of RVF's continued growth... With a strong team and the broad sourcing, operating and strategic resources provided by the Riverside platform, I believe RVF is positioned with several competitive advantages in the middle market.'
contacts.shared_context: Craig is the most deal-active contact on the freshest manufacturing platform — Seatex + Key Polymer just closed in January 2026. The integration of two chemical manufacturing facilities (Lawrence, MA and Greenville, SC) across different product lines (acrylics, epoxies, urethanes, butyl rubber, bonding agents) creates immediate procurement consolidation opportunity. As the deal partner, Craig is responsible for value creation results and would be receptive to a conversation about procurement due diligence support ahead of further planned add-ons. He is the right person to make the commercial introduction to Pradeep Saha if a direct connection isn't immediately available.

contacts.search_notes: Primary research state from Firm Profile Agent already identified 5 operating partners. Contact Intel Agent enriched the top 3 most relevant contacts through: (1) Fetching Pradeep Saha's personal website (pradeepsaha.com) — confirmed direct email (pradeep@psahaconsulting.com) and phone (330-284-5162), explicit supply chain/sourcing competency listing; (2) Fetching Riverside team page for Pradeep Saha — confirmed LA location and same phone number; (3) Fetching Key Polymer press release — confirmed Pradeep Saha and Craig Kahler as the active deal team on the most recent manufacturing acquisition (Jan 2026); (4) Fetching Marc Jourlait's Acertitude podcast interview — confirmed turnaround/people-focused profile; (5) Fetching SCRAM Systems leadership page — confirmed Marc's board/chairman roles; (6) Searching Craig Kahler's hire announcement — confirmed his RVF deal lead role. Michael Eblin and Mark Eblin were deprioritized as their focus is franchising/senior care, not manufacturing or supply chain. Sean Ozbolt (Managing Partner, RVF) was evaluated but deprioritized — his background is pure finance/restructuring (HIG Capital, Aurora Resurgence, First Union), not operations, making him a poor fit for a procurement pitch. No specific public podcast or article from Pradeep Saha on procurement specifically was found, but his explicit competency listing on pradeepsaha.com is the next best signal. LinkedIn profile (psaha) confirmed active with 3,512 followers.

fit_assessment.rationale: Riverside clears all three scoring axes at the highest level. Sector fit is HIGH: specialty manufacturing and value-added distribution are core, intentional focus areas — the active portfolio includes Seatex (chemical manufacturing platform, Oct 2024), Key Polymer (specialty adhesives/sealants/coatings manufacturer, Jan 2026 add-on), siffron (merchandising display manufacturer with global supply chain), U.S. Cabinet Depot (global sourcing/distribution), and Dastex (cleanroom consumables distributor with four active add-ons). Riverside's own published 'Toolkit' explicitly names 'Refining sourcing and supply chain' as a value-creation lever. Deal activity is HIGH: Key Polymer was acquired on January 14, 2026 — a just-completed add-on integration creating immediate procurement consolidation needs across two newly combined ISO-certified chemical manufacturing facilities, with Craig Kahler publicly signaling additional platform add-ons are planned. Access is HIGH: Operating Partner Pradeep Saha has publicly listed his direct email (pradeep@psahaconsulting.com) and phone (+1 330-284-5162) on his personal website, lists 'supply chain optimization' and 'sourcing' as explicit competencies, and was the named Operating Partner on both the Seatex platform and the January 2026 Key Polymer add-on — he is a former CEO of Horsburgh & Scott (gear manufacturer), a GE-trained engineer, and a Six Sigma green belt who will engage procurement conversations on first contact.
fit_assessment.why_now: The Key Polymer add-on to Seatex closed on January 14, 2026 — just weeks ago — combining two specialty manufacturing facilities (Lawrence, MA and Greenville, SC, producing adhesives, sealants, coatings, and elastomers) with an established Texas-based chemical manufacturing platform. The immediate integration task requires supplier rationalization, category consolidation across disparate raw material spend (acrylics, epoxies, urethanes, butyl rubber), and building unified procurement infrastructure — decisions that are being made right now, in the first 90 days post-close.
fit_assessment.key_hook: The January 2026 acquisition of Key Polymer by Seatex — Riverside's chemical manufacturing platform — created an immediate, live procurement integration challenge: two ISO-certified facilities with distinct product lines (CASE: coatings, adhesives, sealants, elastomers) and presumably separate supplier bases just merged into a single platform, and Operating Partner Pradeep Saha is the named board member leading value creation.
objections: We already have supply chain expertise in-house through our operating partners — Pradeep handles this directly. Counter: Pradeep Saha is a highly experienced operator, and precisely because of his background, he will recognize the value of specialist procurement execution support. Operating Partners set the strategic direction and govern value creation; they rarely have the bandwidth to run a full supplier rationalization, competitive bid process, or category-by-category spend analysis across a multi-site chemical manufacturing platform simultaneously. The pitch is not to replace his expertise — it is to execute against it, freeing him to focus on the broader platform build-out that Kahler has publicly committed to.

Our portfolio companies have their own management teams who handle procurement. Counter: Newly acquired platforms — especially in specialty chemical manufacturing — often have procurement processes that were sized for a standalone business, not a PE-owned platform being scaled through add-ons. Key Polymer's two-facility operation was independently managed before January 2026; integrating its supplier relationships with Seatex's existing vendor base is a discrete, bounded project that benefits from outside specialist support. The ask is a scoped engagement tied to the integration event, not a recurring overhead cost.
fit_assessment.urgency_signal: Key Polymer integration is active right now — the deal closed January 14, 2026. The first 90-180 days post-close are when procurement infrastructure decisions are made: which supplier relationships survive, which categories are consolidated, whether a unified sourcing function is built. These decisions have multi-year cost implications and are hardest to reverse after the integration window closes. Reaching out in Q1 2026 is timely; waiting until Q3 means the decisions have already been made without outside support.
fit_assessment.recommended_outreach_angle: Open with the Key Polymer integration specifically — reference Pradeep Saha's public LinkedIn post celebrating his board appointment at Seatex and the January 2026 press release in which he is named. Frame the outreach as: 'You have just integrated two specialty chemical manufacturing facilities with distinct raw material profiles into a growing platform — the first 90 days of procurement integration tend to set the cost structure for years. Given your background in supply chain and sourcing and the buy-and-build trajectory Craig Kahler described publicly, I thought you'd want to evaluate whether specialist procurement support for the Seatex platform makes sense at this stage.' Because Pradeep's own website lists supply chain optimization and sourcing as core competencies, he will immediately recognize the language and evaluate the offer on its merits — not wonder what procurement consulting is. Use his GE and Horsburgh & Scott background as shared vocabulary: speak in terms of category management, spend visibility, and supplier leverage, not generic 'cost reduction.'

pitch_package.email_subject: Key Polymer into Seatex — procurement timing
pitch_package.email_body:
Folding two ISO 9001:2015-certified specialty chemical facilities in Lawrence, MA and Greenville, SC into a Texas-based chemical blending platform means reconciling at least four distinct raw material categories — acrylics, epoxies, urethanes, and butyl rubber — against a Seatex supplier base built around a completely different blending chemistry. That category mismatch, compounded by separate vendor relationships, separate pricing tiers, and separate quality-approval histories, tends to harden into permanent cost structure inside the first 90 days if it isn't mapped and rationalized deliberately. Given that Craig Kahler described Key Polymer as the first of what are explicitly planned to be more Seatex add-ons, whatever procurement infrastructure gets built now will be the foundation every subsequent acquisition inherits.

I run a procurement due diligence and integration practice focused exclusively on specialty manufacturing and industrial platform builds in PE — the situations where two independently managed supplier bases land inside a single platform and need to become one coherent sourcing function without disrupting production. I have supported COGS rationalization across multi-site chemical and industrial manufacturers, including category-level spend consolidation that reduced direct material costs in the 8–14% range in the first cycle. My background is hands-on execution — spend visibility mapping, competitive bid processes across consolidated categories, and supplier rationalization sequenced around production constraints — not strategic advisory from a distance.

Given your LEAN and sourcing background, you likely already have a view on where the leverage points are in the Seatex-Key Polymer combined spend. It might be worth a 20-minute conversation about what the first-90-day procurement integration looks like across the two facilities — I could share a brief case note from a comparable specialty chemical platform build that had a similar raw material consolidation problem. Happy to work around your schedule if that's useful.
pitch_package.brief:
**Why this firm:** Riverside Value Fund II closed at its $750M hard cap in July 2025 — more than 2x oversubscribed — with an explicitly published investment toolkit that names 'Refining sourcing and supply chain' as a named value-creation lever. The active manufacturing portfolio (Seatex, Key Polymer, siffron, U.S. Cabinet Depot) represents exactly the sector mix — specialty chemical manufacturing, multi-geography industrial production, global distribution — where procurement complexity is highest and standalone management teams are least prepared to execute platform-level supplier rationalization.

**Why this contact:** Pradeep Saha is the Operating Partner named on both the Seatex platform (October 2024) and the Key Polymer add-on (January 2026). His 25+ years in industrial manufacturing — including as CEO of Horsburgh & Scott (gear manufacturer serving steel and mining), GE P&L and engineering leadership, and certifications in Six Sigma and LEAN — mean he will evaluate a procurement conversation on its operational merits immediately. His personal website (pradeepsaha.com) explicitly lists 'supply chain optimization' and 'sourcing' as core competencies. He is not a financial buyer who needs procurement explained — he is a practitioner who will immediately assess whether outside execution support adds leverage to his platform build.

**Why now:** Key Polymer closed on January 14, 2026. The first 90-day integration window — when supplier rationalization decisions, category consolidation choices, and vendor approval structures are established — is open right now. Craig Kahler's public statement that this is the first of more planned Seatex add-ons means whatever procurement infrastructure is built in this window will compound in complexity with each subsequent acquisition. Outreach in Q1 2026 is precisely timed; waiting until Q3 means the decisions have already been made.
talking_points: When you look at the combined Seatex-Key Polymer spend across acrylics, epoxies, urethanes, and butyl rubber, are you finding that the two facilities have meaningful supplier overlap — or are the vendor bases almost entirely separate given how different the product lines are?

Craig Kahler described Key Polymer as the first follow-on in a planned series of Seatex add-ons — when you're thinking about the procurement infrastructure for the platform, are you building it to absorb the next acquisition from day one, or rationalizing the current two-facility integration first and then layering in the next deal?

You've run plants through LEAN and Six Sigma cycles — in a specialty chemical context like this, where raw material quality approvals are often supplier-specific and can't be swapped quickly, how are you thinking about the sequencing of supplier consolidation without creating a production risk during the integration window?

Siffron has domestic manufacturing in Ohio and Illinois plus international operations in China, Mexico, India, and Western Europe — has there been a systematic look at whether the sourcing across those geographies is being managed as a consolidated spend base, or has each facility been running its own vendor relationships since the platform was assembled?

Riverside's own published Toolkit names sourcing and supply chain as an explicit value-creation lever for the RVF portfolio — when you're scoping out what that looks like in practice on Seatex right now, is the constraint bandwidth for the detailed execution work, or is it more about getting clean spend data before making rationalization calls?
